description: App layer: Implement getTree HTTP endpoint in KtorGithubGitDataApi, create RepoTreeRepository, wire into DI container, build RepoTreeViewModel and RepoTreeScreen UI, update navigation
steps: 1. Add getTree implementation in app/src/main/java/com/painkiller/data/github/KtorGithubGitDataApi.kt following the existing pattern: override suspend fun getTree(owner: String, repo: String, treeSha: String, recursive: Boolean): CreateTreeResponse = execute { client.get("$baseUrl/repos/$owner/$repo/git/trees/$treeSha") { withBearer(requireToken()); if (recursive) parameter("recursive", "1") } }. Add the ktor parameter import.

2. Create app/src/main/java/com/painkiller/data/github/RepoTreeRepository.kt following MultiFileCommitRepository pattern: class RepoTreeRepository(private val gitDataApi: GithubGitDataApi, private val secureTokenStore: SecureTokenStore). Method loadTree(owner, repo, branch): wraps getRef+getCommit+getTree. Method commitChanges(owner, repo, branch, changes: List<PendingChange>): delegates to RepoTreeOrchestrator. Both check token first.
3. Wire RepoTreeRepository into app/src/main/java/com/painkiller/di/PainkillerContainer.kt as a lazy val repoTreeRepository using gitDataApi and secureTokenStore.

4. Create app/src/main/java/com/painkiller/ui/repotree/RepoTreeViewModel.kt: ViewModel with StateFlow<RepoTreeUiState>. State holds: selectedOwner, selectedRepo, selectedBranch, treeEntries (flat list from recursive tree), currentPath (for breadcrumb navigation), pendingChanges list, isLoading, error, commitResult. Actions: loadTree(), navigateToFolder(path), navigateUp(), addPendingChange(change), removePendingChange(index), commitChanges(). Use ViewModelProvider.Factory companion pattern from AuthViewModel. The ViewModel needs: repoTreeRepository, repoBranchRepository (for repo/branch lists), safFileReader (for uploads).

5. Create app/src/main/java/com/painkiller/ui/repotree/RepoTreeScreen.kt: Composable with Scaffold, TopAppBar with breadcrumb. Content sections: (a) Repo+Branch selector at top (owner/repo text fields or dropdown from repo list, branch picker), (b) Tree list showing files/folders at current path level (click folder = navigate in, long press = context menu with Umbenennen/Verschieben/Loeschen), (c) Action buttons: '+ Neuer Ordner', 'Datei hochladen' (SAF picker), 'ZIP entpacken' (SAF picker for zip), (d) 'Geplante Aenderungen' section showing human-readable pending changes list, (e) 'Auf GitHub speichern' button that triggers commit, (f) Success state showing commit SHA and link. German labels where specified.

6. Update app/src/main/java/com/painkiller/ui/navigation/PainkillerNavGraph.kt: Add ROUTE_REPO_TREE = 'repo_tree' constant. After auth, navigate to ROUTE_REPO_TREE instead of ROUTE_WORKBENCH. Add composable(ROUTE_REPO_TREE) with RepoTreeViewModel wired from container. Keep ROUTE_WORKBENCH composable intact as alternative route (not deleted).
7. Ensure all imports are correct and the app module compiles syntactically (we cannot run assembleDebug locally but code must be valid Kotlin with correct import paths).
acceptance_criteria: KtorGithubGitDataApi.getTree sends GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/trees/{tree_sha}?recursive=1 with bearer auth
RepoTreeRepository exists and delegates to RepoTreeOrchestrator for commits
PainkillerContainer exposes repoTreeRepository as lazy val
RepoTreeViewModel manages tree loading, pending changes, and commit flow via StateFlow
RepoTreeScreen shows repo/branch selector, file tree, action buttons, pending changes, and commit button with German labels
PainkillerNavGraph routes to RepoTreeScreen after auth (ROUTE_WORKBENCH kept as alternative)
Domain module still passes all tests after changes: JAVA_HOME=/opt/toolchains/.local/share/mise/installs/java/17.0.2 ./gradlew :domain:test
